metadata.packName: Browser Agent Orchestration Rules for Banking Finance E5 Simulations
metadata.purpose: Define behavioral rules, probabilities, timing patterns, mistake models, escalation logic, and persona-specific activity tendencies for browser-based agents simulating Microsoft 365 E5 banking/finance activity.

globalOrchestrationPrinciples: Agents should behave like imperfect knowledge workers, not scripts running at constant speed.
Normal work should significantly outnumber risky activity.
Risky actions should be triggered by business pressure, ambiguity, poor permissions, deadline compression, or unclear guidance.
Escalation behavior should occur after warnings, blocked actions, peer feedback, or repeated failures.
The simulation should preserve ambiguity around intent, especially for Devon Reyes.
All generated content, identifiers, recipients, customers, accounts, incidents, and prompts must remain fictional.

timingRules.recommendedRandomization: Use seeded randomness per scenario execution so demos can be repeated while still appearing human.

activityMixTargets.notes: These values are suggested targets across long simulations. Individual scenarios may intentionally increase risk density.

personaBehaviorProfiles.activityStyle: Executive, concise, summary-driven, relies on approved briefings and Copilot summaries.
commonMistakes: Asks Copilot to summarize broad executive content without checking source sensitivity
Forwards a board link to a broader leadership group
Overloads an investigation note with too much technical detail for executives
safeHabits: Escalates to HR and Legal when appropriate

personaBehaviorProfiles.activityStyle: Task-driven, sometimes rushed, uncertain about policy boundaries, prone to shortcuts under pressure.
commonMistakes: Pastes sensitive rows into external AI
Removes or downgrades labels to complete a task
Adds external guest to the wrong channel
safeHabits: Usually frames risky actions as urgent business support
specialRules: Do not make Devon risky in every session
Risk should cluster around deadlines, role-change context, unfamiliar tasks, or similar raw/sanitized file names

personaBehaviorProfiles.activityStyle: Analytical, structured, spreadsheet-heavy, generally careful but frequently handles exports.
commonMistakes: Exports underlying data instead of aggregates
Stores working copies in OneDrive longer than needed
safeHabits: Applies labels to sensitive analytics files

personaBehaviorProfiles.activityStyle: Model-focused, privacy-aware, creates raw and anonymized datasets.
commonMistakes: Keeps pre-anonymized and anonymized files too close together
Sends a clean redline but forgets to verify attachment version

personaBehaviorProfiles.activityStyle: Coordinated, deadline-aware, action-oriented, uses Teams and Planner heavily.
commonMistakes: Adds a guest to simplify coordination
Shares steering materials before final sanitization

mistakeModels.name: Wrong Attachment Selection
mistakeModels.description: User selects an internal or pre-sanitized file instead of the customer-safe version.
mistakeModels.recommendedRemediation: Create clearer naming conventions and require DLP inspection before external send.

mistakeModels.name: Broad Link Creation
mistakeModels.description: User creates a broad organization-wide or external link instead of sharing with named users.
mistakeModels.recommendedRemediation: Prefer named-user links, expiration, and download restrictions for sensitive content.

mistakeModels.name: Unsafe External AI Shortcut
mistakeModels.description: User pastes sensitive rows or case summaries into an unmanaged AI tool to save time.
triggerConditions: User unaware of approved AI workflow
mistakeModels.recommendedRemediation: Coach user to use approved Copilot workflows with sanitized or permission-safe source data.

mistakeModels.name: Label Downgrade Without Sanitization
mistakeModels.description: User changes a file to a lower sensitivity label to share it, but sensitive data remains in the file.
mistakeModels.recommendedRemediation: Require downgrade justification and scan derivative files before sharing.

mistakeModels.name: Endpoint Workaround
mistakeModels.description: User downloads, prints, or copies a file locally to complete work outside the approved workflow.
mistakeModels.recommendedRemediation: Use Endpoint DLP restrictions and provide approved offline review alternatives.

safePromptPatterns: Remove customer identifiers and account-like values.
Create an aggregate summary by segment.
Draft a governance request without raw sensitive values.
riskyPromptPatterns: Find every file containing account-like numbers.
Summarize KYC and AML files by customer.
Rewrite privileged legal notes for PMO action tracking.
Paste raw rows from AML, SAR, treasury, or HR files into external AI.
recommendedRouting: Use CopilotInteraction for approved Microsoft 365 AI activity.
Use AIAppInteraction for unmanaged or external AI activity.
Use SensitiveContentAccessed when prompts reference highly sensitive source files.
Use UnmanagedAppUpload when prompt content includes raw sensitive rows or file uploads.

escalationRules.name: DLP Warning Follow-Up
escalationRules.condition: User receives DLP warning on external send or sharing action.

userBehaviorOptions.behavior: Cancel action and ask security for guidance
Remove attachment and send summary only

escalationRules.name: Blocked External Sharing
escalationRules.condition: DLP blocks external sharing of a regulated file.

userBehaviorOptions.behavior: Ask manager or security why it was blocked
Attempt to share through Teams or OneDrive link

escalationRules.name: Security Investigation Trigger
escalationRules.condition: User produces three or more high-risk signals within 48 hours.

recommendedWeeklySimulationMix.eventDrivenScenarios: 0-1 unless demo requires higher risk density

codexUsageGuidance: Use personaBehaviorProfiles to determine how agents should behave when multiple valid actions are possible.
Use timingRules to insert human-like delays between browser actions.
Use mistakeModels only when scenario context supports a mistake; do not generate random mistakes without narrative cause.
Use escalationRules to determine what users and security teams do after warnings, blocks, and high-risk sequences.
Use scenarioSelectionRules to generate weekly activity plans without overloading the demo with constant high-risk events.
Keep Devon Reyes ambiguous: sometimes rushed, sometimes careless, sometimes safe, and occasionally risky enough to trigger investigation.
